summary: Shapiro shares a tip about finding inexpensive, high-quality drawer rails online as an alternative to the overpriced, low-quality options at Home Depot. The post is a brief 2004-era product recommendation with a link to a hardware supplier. He notes the rails offer full extension, ball bearing movement, and 100-pound capacity for just over $6. He acknowledges he hasn't personally tested them yet but encourages readers to try them.
key_points: Good drawer rails are disproportionately expensive at retail stores like Home Depot
An online hardware supplier offers full-extension ball bearing rails for around $6
The post is a casual product recommendation, not a tested review
money_quotes: Drawer rails are ridiculously expensive, especially for good ones.
Skip Home Depot and get these instead–you'll thank me for it.
key_insight: A short maker-culture product tip from 2004 about finding better drawer hardware online for less money than big-box retail.
